topic: A student finds out their scholarship was meant for someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

Leo stretched languidly, the worn armchair molding perfectly to his frame. Sunlight, thick and golden, slanted across the dusty floorboards of his dorm room, warming the spine of his well-loved paperback. He took a slow, deep breath, letting the tension that had been coiled in his shoulders for weeks simply unwind. He was adrift in a pleasant, quiet hum of contentment. Then, the email landed. A formal notification regarding his prestigious academic grant. His heart gave a sudden, sharp lurch, a feeling entirely out of place in the preceding ease. He clicked it open, his fingers hovering over the screen, a strange stillness settling over him as he read the names listed under the disbursement details. The name wasn't his. It was someone else’s.

[story 2]

Maya leaned back against the cool windowpane, the city noise a distant, muffled murmur beneath her. She traced patterns on the condensation, a slow, rhythmic movement that smoothed away the tight knot in her chest. Everything felt manageable right now; the workload was light, the impending deadlines distant. She was just enjoying the quiet solitude of the late afternoon. But when the official letter arrived, the pleasant haze instantly dissipated. Her gaze skimmed the fine print, and a cold, undeniable clarity washed over her. The scholarship, the one she had worked so tirelessly for, was designated for an entirely different recipient. She slowly folded the paper, the sound crisp in the silence, her jaw tightening almost imperceptibly.

[story 3]

Across the park bench, Sam watched the joggers pass, a faint smile playing on his lips. The air was crisp, carrying the scent of damp earth and distant street food. He felt completely at ease, the weight of the upcoming exams seeming to lift from his shoulders. He was just observing the world, letting his mind wander freely. Then, his phone buzzed with a notification. He picked it up, his thumb moving mechanically over the screen. The details of the scholarship were laid out plainly, and the realization hit him with the force of a physical blow. A slow flush crept up his neck. He lowered the phone, his breathing becoming shallow, and he stared blankly at the green expanse of the lawn, unable to move.
